title: pneumonanthe asclepiadea
intro: Pneumonanthe asclepiadea, also known as lungwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. Traditionally, lungwort has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in mucilages, which have been shown to provide health benefits by soothing irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, and promoting the clearance of mucus from the lungs. Lungwort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions.
